newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The Q1 record print (EPS $2.22, +17% sales, data-center orders +240%, $19.6B committed orders) was reported in early May, and the NVIDIA Beam Rubin DSX collaboration and Boyd Thermal liquid-cooling deal were already known. Stock +2.7% since June 9.

sources.label: Eaton surged on data-center power demand (Yahoo Finance)

verdicts.rankRationale: No fresh fundamental news since June 9 on the grid/switchgear bottleneck. The +240% data-center order growth, $19.6B backlog and NVIDIA Beam Rubin DSX / Boyd Thermal cooling were already in the prior up-call. +2.7% move is drift; the YTD-compression asymmetry that drove the Jun 9 upgrade is now largely captured. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Electrical Americas orders +60% (data-center +240%), 48% backlog growth, NVIDIA HVDC partnership and Boyd liquid-cooling deepen grid-to-chip exposure.
verdicts.neutral: Megatrend fully recognized; YTD +26% leaves less residual under-ownership; execution-dependent backlog conversion.
verdicts.bear: Crowded power-bottleneck trade; data-center capex digestion risk; rich multiple vs. cyclical industrial base.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 orders/backlog and raised guidance; NVIDIA Rubin ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI capex pause or order normalization
